title: Alaska State Troopers commander becomes first Black colonel in state’s history
summary: The promotion of Maurice “Mo” Hughes, who currently heads a Southeast Alaska and Kenai Peninsula detachment, was one of two announced Wednesday.

title: White House puts pause on free at-home COVID tests, bringing access challenges for some
summary: Public health experts say the program’s stoppage will make it harder for the uninsured, those who live in remote areas and people with mobility issues.

title: Watch live at 4 p.m.: Ranked vote results in Alaska’s special U.S. House election
summary: Division of Elections Director Gail Fenumiai will narrate the results as they appear on screen.

title: Wasilla motorcyclist killed in pursuit-related collision with moose at over 100 mph, police say
summary: David Nagl was fleeing officers who tried to pull him over for speeding and failing to stop at a traffic light, according to the Wasilla Police Department.

title: US clears updated COVID boosters targeting omicron variants
summary: The Food and Drug Administration on Wednesday authorized its first update to COVID-19 vaccines, and shots could begin within days.

title: Are you an Alaskan with federal student loans that qualify for debt relief? Share your story.
summary: What does the upcoming plan to forgive federal student loan debt mean for you? Share your story and help us report on this topic.

title: Fire reported at Alaska’s largest prison near Wasilla but no evacuations underway, officials say
summary: No inmates are housed in the area of Goose Creek Correctional Center where the fire started, a corrections department official said.

title: Woman driving stolen truck arrested after Glenn Highway pursuit, police say
summary: Officers deployed a pepper-spray ball and then a dog into the pickup before the arrest, police said.

title: Two people died this month after just one day in Alaska corrections facilities
summary: When asked if sudden deaths raised any red flags, a Department of Corrections spokesperson wrote, “Every death in DOC is of equal concern, and we treat all in-custody deaths with the same thoroughness and sensitivity.”

title: Unvaccinated cadets ordered off Coast Guard Academy campus
summary: The Coast Guard Academy is disenrolling seven cadets for failing to comply with the military’s COVID-19 vaccination mandate, after their requests for religious exemptions were denied.

title: US life expectancy plunged nearly a year in 2021 due to COVID and overdoses
summary: In the first two years of the COVID-19 pandemic, the estimated American lifespan has shortened by nearly three years.

title: Polaris tells snowmachiners not to ride a decade’s-worth of models over fuel tank fire risk
summary: A notice from the company on Tuesday affects around 230,000 Polaris units, including models spanning 2013 to 2023.

title: Seeing brown birch leaves around Alaska? It’s not necessarily a sign of fall — instead, blame leaf-munching larvae.
summary: The caterpillar-like birch leafminer ate its way through birch leaves across Southcentral and the Interior this summer. This summer’s hot and dry weather may have been optimal “conditions for these insects to really go wild,” an entomologist said.

title: North Pole woman arrested on manslaughter charge after man dies of fatal overdose, troopers say
summary: Samantha Pearson told investigators she thought she was selling heroin to the man, who died with high levels of fentanyl in his blood, according to charging documents.

title: Lawsuit challenging Wasilla lawmaker’s eligibility to hold office could be fast-tracked
summary: A former Matanuska-Susitna Borough Assembly member filed the suit in early August, alleging that Eastman’s lifetime membership of the Oath Keepers violates the Alaska Constitution’s disloyalty clause, which has never been tested in court.

title: Republicans in key races scrap online references to Trump, abortion
summary: Attempts by Republicans in competitive contests to pivot away from these issues have emboldened Democrats to mount an aggressive offense.

title: Son of former Alaska state Rep. Dean Westlake indicted on murder charges in his death
summary: Tallon Westlake, 36, was initially charged with manslaughter in what authorities called the beating death of his father.

title: Results in Alaska’s special U.S. House race expected Wednesday when candidates are set to share a stage
summary: Candidates in the special U.S. House race are scheduled to meet in person for a forum in Anchorage Wednesday afternoon. The Alaska Division of Elections is set to release results the same time.

title: Former Soviet leader Mikhail Gorbachev dead at age 91
summary: Gorbachev, the last leader of the Soviet Union, waged a losing battle to salvage a crumbling empire but produced extraordinary reforms that led to the end of the Cold War.

title: Judge delays Georgia Gov. Kemp’s testimony in election probe until after November midterm vote
summary: The judge presiding over the Georgia grand jury investigation into possible election interference by Donald Trump and his allies on Monday denied a motion from Gov. Brian Kemp to quash a subpoena requiring him to testify.

title: Man shot by Anchorage officer charged with attempted murder, kidnapping
summary: Michael Valu ignored police commands Friday morning at Lake Otis Parkway and Tudor Road and pointed a gun at officers, police said.

title: Searchers find body of kayaker on Knik River
summary: Alaska Wildlife Troopers located the remains of Brian Lizer near Wolf Point on Saturday after his kayak overturned while he tried to cross the Knik River.

title: A ‘strange light bouncing up and down in the sky’: The history of UFO sightings in Alaska
summary: In 1947, just as the country was catching flying saucer fever, three Anchorage teens made what is believed to be the state’s first of many sightings.

title: Pakistan flooding deaths pass 1,000 in ‘climate catastrophe’
summary: Flashflooding from the heavy rains has washed away villages and crops assoldiers and rescue workers evacuated stranded residents to the safetyof relief camps and provided food to thousands of displaced Pakistanis.

title: VIDEO: Inside the world’s biggest sockeye salmon run
summary: VIDEO: Inside the world’s biggest sockeye salmon run

title: Salt Lake City police video of fatal encounter with unarmed man shows lack of de-escalation
summary: The death of Nykon Brandon, who was 35, comes as the United States is still seeing uncounted numbers of killings by police of unarmed people, many of whom were suffering a mental health crisis.

title: With a 4-month term, a scramble awaits Alaska’s next US House member
summary: After months of campaigning, a special election, and two weeks of waiting for results, Alaskans will finally learn on Wednesday who will take over the state’s sole seat in the House of Representatives. What happens then?

title: ANALYSIS: Is the United States headed for civil war?
summary: Fighting words and extremism are on the rise. We are not yet in “Turner Diaries” territory, but that doesn’t mean the country will avoid violent conflict.

title: Charlie Pierce, Kenai Peninsula mayor, says he will resign to focus on governor bid
summary: Pierce and others decline to discuss closed-door meeting that preceded Friday’s announcement

title: Campaign roundup: What’s happening in Alaska’s elections for Senate, House, governor and legislature
summary: With primary voting in the rear-view mirror, candidates in Alaska are campaigning in full swing.

title: Anchorage’s school bus driver shortage: How it unfolded and what we know so far
summary: Anchorage School District officials believe they’ll have full bus driver staffing by the beginning of October, given how many people are currently in the training and hiring process.

title: Biden administration to appoint Arctic ambassador
summary: The ambassador will engage with other Arctic nations and Indigenous groups to promote U.S. interests in the region.

title: Student vaccinations slowed during covid. Can schools catch them up?
summary: As pediatric visits fell off during the pandemic, fewer students got routine vaccines like those for measles and polio.

title: Coast Guard, troopers investigating boat’s close pass to floatplane in Halibut Cove
summary: A pilot described being approached by a boat while he taxied out of the cove across Kachemak Bay before it made several extremely close passes in what he called a ‘dangerous maneuver.’

title: Chris Bye will likely advance in Alaska’s U.S. House election. But he’s not canceling his fishing trips.
summary: Libertarian Chris Bye has less than 1% of the vote in the U.S. House primary. His name is likely to be on the November ballot, but the river guide is sticking to his fishing plans.

title: Mat-Su school district’s contractor moves past ‘unacceptable’ start short on drivers — and buses
summary: District officials now say they were tracking eight or 10 unaccounted-for students on a chaotic first day of school last week as Durham School Services started a new contract.

title: Secret Service recovers $286M in stolen COVID pandemic loans
summary: An investigation found that alleged conspirators submitted Economic Injury Disaster Loan applications by using fake or stolen employment and personal information.

title: Attacks on Ukraine train system may be war crimes, experts say
summary: Russia has offered little evidence to back up its claim that Ukrainian troops were the target of its attack on the Chaplyne railway station on Wednesday.

title: Student loan forgiveness application coming in October, White House says
summary: White House National Economic Council deputy director Bharat Ramamurti said Friday the application will be ready in early October and borrowers should see relief with four to six weeks.

title: Restored power at Ukraine nuclear plant paves way for U.N. visit
summary: The fight for control of Europe’s largest nuclear plant is a proxy for the larger war as Ukraine seeks to oust occupying Russian forces and Moscow aims to annex the area.

title: Read the full but redacted Mar-a-Lago search affidavit
summary: Why did FBI agents search former president Donald Trump’s residence?

title: Controlling inflation will cause ‘some pain’ for households and businesses, Federal Reserve chief says
summary: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell delivered a stark message Friday: The Fed will likely impose more large interest rate hikes in coming months and is resolutely focused on taming the highest inflation in four decades.

title: Moderna sues Pfizer over patents behind COVID-19 vaccine
summary: Moderna is suing Pfizer and the German drugmaker BioNTech, accusing its main competitors of copying Moderna’s technology to make their own vaccine.

title: U.K. residents face 80% spike in energy bills as war in Ukraine drives up natural gas prices
summary: Britain has the highest inflation rate among the Group of Seven wealthiest democracies and has experienced disruptive strikes for months as workers push for pay to keep pace with the cost of living.

title: Alaska sees sharp drop in child vaccinations, prompting concern about future outbreaks of preventable diseases
summary: Alaska’s top health officials said they were concerned that low vaccine coverage rates could lead to a resurgence of diseases such as polio, measles and mumps.

title: During Anchorage School District’s bus driver shortage, 4 trainees can’t wait to take the wheel
summary: “We used to be kids too,” said Javier Tatham, one of the district’s drivers in training. “Now it’s our turn to help out the community.”

title: Friends remember Anchorage car crash victims as kind and giving student-athletes
summary: Amelia Nowak, 18, and Derek Duerr, 20, who died in a fiery vehicle crash south of downtown, were about to start their college careers in Washington state.

title: Three more Republican-led states enact abortion ‘trigger laws’
summary: To date, 13 states have passed so-called trigger laws that were designed to outlaw almost all abortions if the high court threw out the constitutional right to end a pregnancy.

title: Environmental groups sue to stop federal approval of exploration at Alaska oil project
summary: The groups contend a federal agency did not consider the full effects of greenhouse-gas emissions associated with the potential 1.6 billion barrels of oil the Peregrine prospect is estimated to contain.

title: Pentagon plan aims to reduce civilian deaths caused by airstrikes
summary: The Pentagon’s plan for reducing civilian casualties follows a series of deadly mistakes, and would affect planning for drone strikes and other operations.

title: Environmental groups sue to stop federal approval of exploration at Alaska oil project
summary: The groups contend a federal agency did not consider the full effects of greenhouse-gas emissions associated with the potential 1.6 billion barrels of oil the Peregrine prospect is estimated to contain.

title: Student loan relief plan highlights burden on Black borrowers
summary: Black borrowers on average carry about $40,000 in federal student loan debt, $10,000 more than white borrowers. The disparity reflects a racial wealth gap in the U.S. — one that some advocates say the debt relief plan does not do enough to narrow.

title: Anchorage Assembly overrides Mayor Bronson’s vetoes on formation of homelessness task force and federal relief funds
summary: The Assembly restored all federal COVID-19 relief funding for projects related to homelessness, including millions for the purchase of a hotel or other buildings for low-income and supportive housing. The Assembly also reinstated a task force to come up with a plan to shelter people experiencing homelessness this winter.

title: Nebraska school officials close student newspaper after LGBTQ articles
summary: District officials have not said when or why the decision was made to eliminate the student paper, but an email from a school employee cancelling the paper’s printing services said it was “because the school board and superintendent are unhappy with the last issue’s editorial content.”

title: 24 states share $560 million from feds for high-priority cleanup of abandoned oil and gas wells
summary: Alaska officials said the state would plug and clean up 12 to 18 wells with its initial grant.

title: Palmer man convicted on federal murder charges linked to string of Mat-Su robberies
summary: John Pearl Smith II attempted to steal drugs from Mat-Su homes in 2015 and 2016 and killed two people in 2016, prosecutors say.

title: Millions in COVID aid went to retrain veterans. Only 397 landed jobs.
summary: The program - part of the American Rescue Plan - fell victim to a host of problems: hasty design, poor timing, and inadequate promotion and misuse of VA money.

title: Tracking COVID-19 in Alaska: State reports increase in cases compared with previous week
summary: Statewide, 67.8% of Alaskans six months and older had received at least one dose of the COVID-19 vaccine.

title: Use your currant and blueberry harvest in this no-churn ice cream for an irresistible treat
summary: Combining heavy whipping cream and sweetened condensed milk give this dessert a creaminess that will dispatch your ice cream maker to a darkened corner of the cupboard.

title: All the rain isn’t bothering your plants, and it shouldn’t bother you either
summary: Rain may put a bit of a damper on your day, but it brings plenty of benefits to your plants, lawns and vegetables.

title: 17-year-old pilot who stopped in Alaska sets record for solo flight around world
summary: Mack Rutherford’s sister, Zara, who finished her own trip global flight in January at age 19, previously held the ultralight record.

title: ‘Ignoring the will of voters’: Groups demand progress on body cameras for Anchorage police
summary: Civil rights organizations say they’re considering legal action after a 2021 voter-approved tax increase to fund the technology has yet to yield results.

title: Biden to cancel up to $10,000 in student debt for most borrowers, extend payment pause
summary: More than 40 million Americans could see their student loan debt reduced — and in many cases eliminated — under the long-awaited forgiveness plan.

title: Maintenance error likely cause of engine scare on Alaska Airlines 737 with 182 people aboard, expert says
summary: When the pilots turned back to Seattle, the engines covers ripped off completely on landing and pieces hit the fuselage.

title: 15 reported killed in independence day attack on Ukrainian train station, authorities say
summary: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy had warned for days that Russia might attempt “something particularly cruel” this week as Ukrainians celebrate their independence day.

title: America’s No. 1 homelessness problem: Knowing who is actually homeless
summary: Governments nationwide spend billions of dollars to address homelessness. But advocates and academics agree they’re relying on incomplete data. Seattle is testing a new method to find them.

title: Marijuana use among young people in U.S. at record high, study says
summary: About 1 in 10 young people used cannabis on a near-daily basis, according to a study funded by the National Institutes of Health.

title: Alaska election officials sued over lack of ballot curing process
summary: The complainants noted that 7,468 ballots were rejected from Alaska’s first all by-mail election, or 4.5% of the total votes cast, with a greater proportion of those rejected ballots coming from parts of the state where Alaska Natives make up a majority of the population.

title: City removes Anchorage HR director from library staff complaint investigations after concerns over ‘I’m with Judy’ T-shirt
summary: Anchorage is removing Human Resources Director Niki Tshibaka from any involvement with personnel complaints filed by Anchorage Public Library staff after staff raised concerns that the department would not investigate “thoroughly, fairly, and impartially,” according to a memo from the city’s ombudsman.

title: Ukraine fears stepped-up attacks around national holiday
summary: Fear is mounting in Ukraine that Russia may try to spoil Ukraine’s Independence Day holiday and mark the six-month point in the war by intensifying its attacks.

title: FBI’s Mar-a-Lago search followed months of resistance, delay by Trump
summary: Former president Donald Trump’s advisers pushed back this spring against requests for the FBI to review highly classified documents sent back from Mar-a-Lago.

title: Inmate dies at Hiland Mountain Correctional Center within a day of her arrival
summary: Nastashia Minock was arrested Monday and died early Tuesday morning, the Alaska Department of Corrections said. Foul play is not suspected, officials say.

title: Atlanta officers who shot Rayshard Brooks won’t face charges
summary: The Atlanta officers who shot Rayshard Brooks, a 27-year-old Black American man, during the height of 2020 racial justice protests will not face charges.

title: Ukraine nuclear plant worker killed by Russian mortar as tensions rise
summary: The second killing of an employee at the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant comes as the U.N. Security Council meets to discuss the perilous situation.

title: Tara Sweeney drops out of Alaska U.S. House race
summary: Republican Tara Sweeney dropped out of the U.S. House race after garnering around 3.7% of votes counted so far.

title: 4 Alaska legislative candidates withdraw from general election
summary: Candidates can withdraw from the general election before a Sept. 5 deadline

title: Former Louisville officer pleads guilty to federal conspiracy charge in Breonna Taylor case
summary: Federal investigators said Kelly Goodlett added a false line to the warrant and later conspired with another detective to create a cover story when Taylor’s March 13, 2020, shooting death by police began gaining national attention.

title: What do you want to know about homelessness in Anchorage?
summary: Help shape our coverage.

title: Your first brush with coronavirus could affect how a fall booster works
summary: As omicron-specific vaccines near, scientists debate how “original antigenic sin” will influence immune responses

title: Alaska Airlines flight returns to Sea-Tac due to ‘unusual vibration’
summary: Part of the cowling covering an engine detached from the planeduring landing, according to a statement from the airline.

title: Biden nears decision on canceling $10,000 in student loans as allies feud
summary: Borrowers are scheduled to resume student loan payments Aug. 31 unless White House extends the current pause.

title: Young couple killed in fiery wreck near downtown Anchorage by man who didn’t remember driving, charges say
summary: An 18-year-old woman and a 20-year-old man died early Sunday after their car was rear-ended by a driver who told police he was intoxicated, charges said.

title: Young couple killed in fiery wreck near downtown Anchorage by man who didn’t remember driving, charges say
summary: An 18-year-old woman and a 20-year-old man died early Sunday after their car was rear-ended by a driver who told police he was intoxicated, charges said.

title: Anchorage Assembly to debate Mayor Bronson’s proposal for voters to decide whether city clerk should be an elected office
summary: Assembly leadership is opposing the mayor’s proposal, pointing to the potential for added political pressure to a key nonpartisan city position. But the mayor contends it will increase public trust and bring added accountability to the clerk’s office.

title: Anchorage man takes Seward Silver Salmon Derby
summary: Steve Oyler has been fishing in the derby since the 1980s. He caught the winning fish on the day his granddaughters turned 10 years old.

title: Bodies of 2 Noorvik residents found in Kobuk River after discovery of overturned skiff
summary: Henry Black and Diane Coffin were reported missing Friday after they left Kotzebue but never arrived in Noorvik, Alaska State Troopers say.

title: Trump seeks special master to review seized Mar-a-Lago materials
summary: In a court filing, Trump’s lawyers accused the Justice Department of treating the former president unfairly for years, and they want the DOJ to better explain why a search was necessary.

title: Asylum seekers caught in political battle over immigration in New York City, Washington
summary: Thousands of asylum seekers from across Latin America and the Caribbean are getting caught in the political battle over U.S. immigration policy after two Republican governors started sending busloads of migrants to New York City and Washington.

title: Entire election staff abruptly quits over threats, upending a rural Texas county
summary: The departures pile on the examples across the U.S. of how death threats, harassment and unfounded accusations have driven local election officials from their jobs.

title: Photos: Alaska Hmong New Year celebration in downtown Anchorage
summary: “I really want everyone in the community to see the Hmong culture, the tradition, our clothing. But importantly, for our children to see what their culture is about,” an organizer said.

summary: Workplace advice: The trend emerged in July from a viral TikTok video.

title: Son of former Alaska state Rep. Dean Westlake charged in his death
summary: Westlake’s son, Tallon Westlake, 36, was charged Sunday with manslaughter and tampering with evidence.

title: Car bomb outside Moscow kills daughter of key Putin ally
summary: The daughter of influential Russian political theorist Alexander Dugin, often referred to as “Putin’s brain,” was killed in the explosion on the outskirts of Moscow, authorities said Sunday.

title: At the peak of his powers, legendary comedian Bob Hope made multiple visits to Alaska
summary: Histories of Anchorage: The famous performer made numerous stops in Alaska to entertain troops as part of USO tours and even filmed an episode of his TV show here.

title: Alaska Native linguists create a digital Inupiaq dictionary, combining technology, accessibility and language preservation
summary: Iñupiaq linguist Edna Ahgeak Paniattaaq MacLean and two Yup’ik web developers, Christopher Egalaaq Liu and Lonny Alaskuk Strunk, worked on a project to make learning the language in school and at home faster, easier and more accessible, even in rural areas.

title: I’ve been with my partner for 12 years and he still hasn’t divorced his ex. Is it time for me to walk away?
summary: Wayne and Wanda realize that sometimes the time and energy invested in a relationship make it difficult to make a change, but one may be necessary.

title: Wasilla elementary school closes suddenly Friday due to staffing shortfall
summary: School employees including people in “key positions” were out sick, a Matanuska-Susitna Borough School District spokeswoman said.

title: Mat-Su school transportation crisis prompts cancellation of sports buses
summary: Schools this week told families they would provide an update to the situation in mid-September.

title: Aurora brightens northern skies, with more weekend activity possible
summary: Dancing auroras lit up skies from North America to Europe over the past few days. Minor to moderate activity is expected to continue over the next few nights.

title: ADN Politics podcast: A very special primary
summary: Listen: ADN journalists analyze what happened in Alaska’s Aug. 16 election — and what happens next.

title: ADN Politics podcast: Trump comes to Alaska
summary: Listen: Earlier this summer, former President Donald Trump made good on his vow to come to Alaska. ADN journalists unpack what happened at his first rally in the state and what his endorsement means in Alaska’s races for U.S. House, Senate and governor.

title: Students head back to school in Anchorage on a first day that felt ‘more normal’
summary: For many, the first day back wasn’t without its hiccups, though, as the Anchorage School District grapples with disruptions to school bus service for thousands of students.

title: Alaska budget surplus shrinks with falling oil prices
summary: When Gov. Mike Dunleavy signed the budget in late June, he referred to a recent revenue forecast that showed the state was set to deposit $1.6 billion into the Constitutional Budget Reserve, the state’s main savings account. Since then, the oil revenue forecast is down by roughly $1.4 billion.

title: Judge blocks Florida’s ‘Stop Woke Act’ restrictions for private companies
summary: In a ruling that took aim at one of Gov. Ron DeSantis’s top priorities, a U.S. District Court judge said Florida has turned “the First Amendment upside down” by trying to regulate how employers train employees on topics such as racial inclusion and gender equity.

title: Where to get a monkeypox vaccine in Anchorage this week
summary: Shots are available Friday at a local infectious disease doctor’s office and daily at a clinic in Muldoon.

title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy faces unprecedented criticism over failure to warn of war
summary: Until this week, Ukrainians seemed to see President Volodymyr Zelenskyy as beyond reproach. Comments he made to the The Washington Post punctured the bubble.

title: From missed stops to missing children, Mat-Su families report busing chaos as school begins
summary: A new contractor, Durham School Services, has a 10-year contract to handle transportation for the Matanuska-Susitna Borough School District starting this year.

title: Driver killed in head-on collision with RV on Parks Highway
summary: The collision occurred after a Ford SUV crossed into oncoming traffic north of Willow Thursday morning, according to Alaska State Troopers.

title: Blast kills 21 in Kabul mosque, including influential cleric
summary: Since the Taliban takeover in Afghanistan, there has been a surge of attacks by a branch of the Islamic State.

title: Glaciers in Europe are experiencing the most severe melting on record
summary: While European glaciers have been shrinking for decades, data and field reports show that the melting this summer is the most severe on record. Some glaciers have melted one to two months faster than normal.

title: Texas school district’s book purge takes Bible, Anne Frank story and scores of other books off shelves
summary: A day before school began for its approximately 35,000 students, Keller Independent School District announced a last-minute review of scores of books that had been challenged in the previous school year.

title: Mount Kilimanjaro gets the internet, a gift to those climbing for the ’gram
summary: Tanzania’s information minister urged the state-run internet provider to extend its operations to other tourist sites and national parks. The internet project is partly supported by China.

title: Murkowski leads Trump-backed Tshibaka in Alaska’s U.S. Senate primary
summary: In a primary race largely seen as a referendum on former President Trump, U.S. Sen. Lisa Murkowski was leading challenger Kelly Tshibaka in early results Wednesday. The top four vote getters will advance to the November general election.

title: Amber Alert canceled after 15-year-old girl found ‘safe,’ troopers say
summary: Steven Narron, 51, was in custody Thursday morning, troopers said. Alexis Bane was believed to have been with Narron when the alert was issued.

title: Gas-powered muscle cars speed toward the sunset, to be replaced by EVs
summary: One reason for the industry shift is that electric vehicles are simply faster off the starting line. Their handling is typically better too.

title: More Anchorage school bus drivers are on the way, but service disruptions are the reality for now

title: Anchorage police chief says department will reevaluate its policy on officers carrying Narcan
summary: “Going forward, we will probably have a change in our status on whether we’re carrying Narcan or not,” Anchorage Police Chief Michael Kerle said during a public safety meeting this week.

title: New head librarian declines to take job at embattled Anchorage library
summary: Mayor Bronson’s nominee has opted not to accept the city’s employment offer. A spokesman for the mayor said the city will repost the position.

title: Senate Democrats say they’ve reached agreement on tax, climate package
summary: Arizona Sen. Kyrsten Sinema, a centrist who was seen as the pivotal vote, says in a statement that she has agreed to changes in the measure’s tax and energy provisions and is ready to “move forward” on the bill.

title: Driver shortage could lead to bus route suspensions for Anchorage students, superintendent says
summary: It was not immediately clear which routes would be impacted or how the district would decide where to pause services, if needed.

title: New head librarian declines to take job at embattled Anchorage library
summary: Mayor Bronson’s nominee has opted not to accept the city’s employment offer. A spokesman for the mayor said the city will repost the position.

title: Conspiracy theorist Alex Jones ordered to pay Sandy Hook parents more than $4M
summary: The jury’s decision Thursday marks the first time the Infowars host has been held financially liable for falsely claiming that the attack that killed 20 children and six educators in Newtown, Connecticut, was staged.

title: Craft beer is polarizing: More drinkers want high ABV or none at all
summary: Growth in both high-strength and non-alcoholic beers is putting 4% to 6% workhorse craft beers in peril.

title: A mayor in England is fighting for his office. He’s also a pony.
summary: Supporters of Patrick the pony mayor say the complaint lodged against his outdoor pen was meant to take him down.

title: Tracking COVID-19 in Alaska: State reports more cases but fewer hospitalizations than previous week
summary: The state health department did not report any new COVID-19 deaths.

title: Invasive trees and varieties prone to infestation are on the way out. But what should they be replaced with?
summary: Alaska gardening columnist Jeff Lowenfels offers a rundown of the best options for replacing a tree or just adding a new one.

title: Egg tortilla rolls make a great quick breakfast or a snack on the go
summary: Both simple to make and adaptable to your favorite flavors, the rolls can be extravagant or spartan.

title: Russian judge convicts WNBA’s Griner, sentencing her to 9 years in prison
summary: While recapping the evidence and giving her findings Thursday, the judge said the 31-year-old Griner illegally brought drugs into Russia.

title: Homeland Security watchdog previously accused of misleading investigators, report says
summary: Joseph V. Cuffari, now under scrutiny for his handling of deleted Secret Service text messages from the Jan. 6 attack, had been accused of running “afoul” of ethics regulations while he was a federal agent.

title: Indiana Rep. Walorski, 2 staffers killed in head-on crash
summary: Republican U.S. Rep. Jackie Walorski was killed Wednesday in acar crash in her northern Indiana district along with two members of hercongressional staff and another person, police said.

title: Seven years of sex abuse: How Mormon officials let it happen
summary: TheAssociated Press has obtained nearly 12,000 pages of sealed records, which offer the most detailed and comprehensivelook yet at a so-called help line that families of survivorssay is part of a system that caneasily be misused by church leaders to divert abuse accusations awayfrom law enforcement.

title: After Supreme Court ruling, it’s open season on US gun laws
summary: The Supreme Court ruling expanding gun rightsthreatens to upend firearms restrictions across the country asactivists wage court battles over everything from bans on AR-15-styleguns to age limits.

title: ‘An unmistakable message.’ Kansas’ vote for abortion rights sends ripples nationwide
summary: Kansas’ strong vote in favor of abortion rights –coming in a state long dominated by Republicans – has Democrats andabortionrights supporters ready to go on the offensive in theNovember election, encouraged by an unexpectedly commanding victory.

title: Windy the wolf dies at 16, marking the end of an era at Alaska Zoo
summary: Windy was the last member of a six-wolf pack adopted in 2006, and helped educate millions on lupine behavior and ways.

title: In scientific advance, cells in pig organs revived an hour after death
summary: The research challenges the assumption that tissue death is swift and irreversible, as the scientists were able to restore the function of several organs an hour after the pigs’ hearts had stopped.

title: Democrats face blowback after boosting far-right Michigan candidate
summary: Democrats this year have tried to interfere in multiple GOP primaries, using ads that appear to be attacks on more extreme candidates as a way to subtly promote those contenders — the idea being they will be more easily beatable in the general election.

title: Deadly California wildfire razes scenic river town
summary: About 100 buildings ranging from homes to greenhouses have burned in the McKinney Fire and at least four bodies have been found in the Klamath River region.

title: 70 deaths, many wasted organs are blamed on transplant system errors
summary: An investigation by the Senate Finance Committee blamed the fatalities on errors in screening organs for disease, blood-type mix-ups and other mistakes

title: ‘Never seen it this bad’: America faces catastrophic teacher shortage
summary: School officials everywhere are scrambling to ensure that, as students return to classrooms, someone will be there to educate them.

title: Takeaways from primaries in Kansas, Michigan, Missouri and Arizona
summary: If nothing else, this primary season has shown that, for many Republicans, denying 2020′s election results is the price for entry.

title: Pelosi says US will not abandon Taiwan as China steps up military exercises
summary: U.S. House Speaker Nancy Pelosi left Taiwan after a visit that heightened tensions with China, saying Wednesday that she and other members of Congress in her delegation showed they will not abandon their commitment to the self-governing island.

title: Kansas voters resoundingly support protecting access to abortion
summary: Voters rejected a ballot measure that would have allowed the Republican-controlled Legislature to tighten restrictions or ban the procedure outright.

title: Pelosi to meet with Taiwan’s biggest semiconductor manufacturer
summary: The meeting with the CEO of TSMC, the largest computer chip manufacturer in the world, underscores the importance of the tiny silicon bits to the United States.

title: Q&A with Alaska U.S. House candidates: Do you support gun control legislation?
summary: The Anchorage Daily News asked candidates for U.S. House running in the special primary election to answer a series of questions. Read all of their responses here.

title: State of Alaska proposes major overhaul of Seward Highway intersection near Girdwood
summary: The proposal includes turnoffs looping over wetlands, a bridge over the Seward Highway and a roundabout to slow traffic into town.

title: More people are catching coronavirus a second time, heightening long COVID risk, experts say
summary: Earlier in the pandemic, it was assumed that getting infected afforded some degree of lasting protection, for perhaps a few months. As the coronavirus mutates, that’s no longer a given.

title: Supreme Court certifies ruling ending Trump-era border policy
summary: About 70,000 migrants were subject to the policy, known officially as “Migrant Protection Protocols” from when former President Donald Trump introduced it in January 2019 until President Joe Biden suspended it on his first day in office.

title: It’s a bird, it’s a plane! It’s a fish? On Alaska’s history of collisions between planes and animals
summary: History column: Despite the fact that the state’s only known plane-fish collision was reported in the newspaper April 1, it was not a joke.
